topic: CP trajectory for US negative GDP Q1-Q3 2026
summary: CP has been remarkably stable at 0.27-0.28 for past 2+ weeks. Current CP is 0.28, just above the 0.27 threshold. 778 forecasters = very stable base.
content: The CP for post 41089 (Will US experience negative GDP growth Q1/Q2/Q3 2026) has been oscillating between 0.27 and 0.28 since ~Jan 21. Before that, it ranged from 0.22-0.33 in late Dec/early Jan. The question has 778 forecasters, making it very stable. The threshold for the meta-question is 0.27 (strictly higher than). Current CP = 0.28. Of the last 10 readings, 6 are above 0.27, 4 are at 0.27. The CP rarely dips below 0.27 (only 2 of 50 recent readings were below 0.27, both 0.25-0.26). Recent trend (Feb 1-3) shows slight upward drift to 0.28-0.29. Economic context: Q1 2025 GDP was -0.6%, Q2 recovered to +3.8%. Tariffs continue to weigh on outlook. Goldman Sachs above-consensus growth forecast, but tariff uncertainty persists. The underlying question uses BEA third estimate, covering Q1-Q3 2026.
